title: System Identification (N4SID/ARX)
source_data_quality_note: Source MD is a widefield-fluorescence fan-out artifact; principle grounded in domain literature.

E.description: System Identification (N4SID/ARX): System identification: estimate state-space matrices (A,B,C,D) or ARX coefficients from input-output measurements. The forward operator produces the measurement through a 3-node primitive DAG (S.n4sid.subspace_id…); recovery is posed as a parameter_estimation problem. Difficulty tier delta=3 with effective condition number kappa_eff~100; undermodeling_order_mismatch, feedback_in_data set the accuracy floor at the Omega boundary. See the forward_model field for the closed-form equation.
E.forward_model: y(k) = C*x(k) + D*u(k) + e(k); x(k+1) = A*x(k) + B*u(k) + w(k); N4SID via block Hankel matrix SVD
E.world_state_x: system matrices (A,B,C,D) of order n, input delay

W.regime: Existence of the recovered system_matrices_ABCD is guaranteed within the declared Omega bounds. Uniqueness holds on the measurement-supported subspace; out-of-support modes are controlled by declared priors. Stability is conditionally stable (kappa_eff ~= 100); undermodeling_order_mismatch dominates the stability cliff; the remaining mismatch parameters contribute higher-order bias terms. Gaussian sets the irreducible data-fidelity floor.

C.error_bound: Data-fidelity bound: model fit > 80% for good data; N4SID optimal for large N. Noise floor: O(sigma / sqrt(kappa_eff)) with kappa_eff~100. Mismatch-induced bias + C2 * |undermodeling_order_mismatch|; iterative solvers achieve q=2 convergence (O(1/k^2)) near the optimum; epsilon_fn captures quality degradation across declared Omega.
C.complexity: O(N * n ** 2) for subspace methods per iteration
